title: الاضطراب العاطفي الشديد وانتكاساته الحادة في الأطفال والadolescents
text_original: and • Outbursts and tantrums that have been happening emotional and have an intense temper outburst regularly for at least 12 months with yelling or hitting. A child with DMDD • Chronically irritable or angry mood most of the day, experiences these intense temper outbursts a nearly every day few times a week. • Trouble functioning due to irritability in more than one place, such as at home, at school, or with peers How is DMDD diagnosed? If you think your child may be experiencing symptoms of DMDD, talk to your child’s health care provider. Describe your child’s behavior, and report what you have observed and learned from talking with others, such as their teacher or counselor. An evaluation by your child’s health care provider can help clarify problems underlying your child’s behavior, and the provider may recommend next steps. You can also ask your health care provider for a referral to a mental health professional with experience working with children and adolescents. DMDD symptoms also can occur at the same time as other disorders associated with irritability, such as attention-deficit/ hyperactivity disorder (ADHD) or anxiety disorders. An accurate diagnosis is vital for effective treatment. Cognitive behavioral therapy (CBT) targets the relationship between thoughts, behaviors, and feelings For more information about the diagnosis and and is often effective in treating anger and disruptive treatment of children, visit www.nimh.nih.gov/ behavior. Researchers at NIMH are also using CBT to childrenandmentalhealth. If you need help help children increase their ability to tolerate frustration
text_simplified: يواجه الطفل اضطرابات عاطفية شديدة وانتكاسات حادة، وقد تستمر هذه الاضطرابات لسنوات طويلة.
